title: Okonomiyaki

overview.description: A savoury Japanese cabbage pancake with bacon, spring onions and a light batter, topped with classic okonomiyaki garnishes.

method: Whisk together the flour and baking powder.
Gradually whisk in the stock until smooth.
Fold in the cabbage, spring onions and bacon.
Heat a lightly oiled frying pan over a medium heat.
Pour in half the mixture and shape into a round approximately 2cm thick.
Cook for 5–6 minutes until golden underneath.
Cook for another 5–6 minutes until cooked through.
Sprinkle with aonori and bonito flakes if using.

learning.did-you-know: Okonomiyaki translates roughly as 'grilled how you like it', reflecting its adaptable nature.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a light savoury batter, cook a thick pancake evenly and balance classic Japanese toppings.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick weeknight meal that turns inexpensive cabbage into something completely different and encourages using pantry staples.
chapman-tips: Don't overmix the batter once the cabbage is added.
Finely shred the cabbage so it softens as the pancake cooks.
Cook over a medium heat—too hot and the outside burns before the centre is cooked.
Two spatulas make flipping much easier.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in a dry frying pan to restore a little crispness.
